seed: desire vs restraint; near_vs_far; spiral_pressure; sweep; lattice against pressure_band; sealed_edges; saturated_vs_muted; diamond; love_betrayal | abstract variation request 424; target batch count 500
title: Tension of Emotion
reasoning: This abstract artwork embodies the conflict between desire and restraint through a deliberate color palette and spatial arrangement. The warm hues of red oxide and small orange represent desire, pulsing through the grid in a ladder-like pattern, attempting to overflow beyond the sealed edges. In contrast, the cooler shades of blackened plum and muted cream create a flame-like pattern, symbolizing the restraint that seeks to contain this warm pull. The interplay of these patterns creates a visual tension that captures the essence of conflict, echoing the emotional depth of the seed. The main collision occurs centrally, where warm and cool tones are nearest, creating a dynamic center that draws the eye. Negative space is preserved to imply constriction and choice, while broken pixels introduce an element of imperfection, reinforcing the themes of love and betrayal.

layout.name: threshold
layout.description: The canvas is divided by a passage or seam where the two emotions meet.

composition.name: overflow_vs_containment
composition.idea: Bright or hot marks exceed a boundary while cooler marks try to contain them.

referenceExample.title: Desire Held Back
referenceExample.pattern: warm saturated pull stopped by a cool sealed boundary

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
